summary: Excellent soundboard mix with a standout Dark Star → Morning Dew sequence; minor speed‑correction quirks but overall great performance.
review: The recording captures the May 23, 1972 show on a clean soundboard source, praised for its excellent mix and relatively forward vocals, though a slight speed correction introduces a tiny pitch shift and a hint of tape hiss. Musically the night shines thanks to an iconic Dark Star that flows into a flawless Morning Dew, with Jerry’s guitar accents and Phil’s bass tone highlighted, plus a superb Two Souls in Communion. Overall the show is regarded as one of the era’s best despite a few technical nitpicks.

ai_rating.rationale: Three strong positive reviews (two 4‑star, one 5‑star) praise both performance and recording; only a single low‑rating meta comment does not detract from the overall assessment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mix described as excellent with less distant vocals; minor speed‑correction (~1% fast) causes slight pitch/intona­tion shift and a bit of tape hiss/distortion, but overall clear and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is praised for its seamless segue from Dark Star into Morning Dew and a consistently strong performance throughout the show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar accents and solo on Dark Star are highlighted as exemplary, reinforcing his status as a great lead guitarist/singer.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass tone on Dark Star receives special praise for its clarity and musicality.
